content.problemStatement:
Sushil has an array AAA of NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​. He constructs a new array from it, the prefix sum array P=[P1,P2,...,PN]P = [P_1, P_2, ..., P_N]P=[P1​,P2​,...,PN​], where Pi=A1+A2+...+AiP_i = A_1 + A_2 + ... + A_iPi​=A1​+A2​+...+Ai​, i.e. the sum of the first iii elements.

Sushil hates even numbers but he likes odd numbers, and thus wants to sure there are as many odd numbers as possible in the prefix sum array PPP. He is allowed to play with the array AAA and rearrange it arbitrarily. Find the maximum number of odd numbers in the prefix sum array PPP if Sushil rearranges the array AAA optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible number of odd numbers in the prefix sum array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The optimal rearrangement is [1,4,2,3][1, 4, 2, 3][1,4,2,3]. The constructed prefix sum array is as follows :

P1=A1=1P_1 = A_1 = 1P1​=A1​=1
P2=A1+A2=5P_2 = A_1 + A_2 = 5P2​=A1​+A2​=5
P3=A1+A2+A3=7P_3 = A_1 + A_2 + A_3 = 7P3​=A1​+A2​+A3​=7
P4=A1+A2+A3+A4=10P_4 = A_1 + A_2 + A_3 + A_4 = 10P4​=A1​+A2​+A3​+A4​=10

It has only 111 even number and 333 odd numbers. It can be proven that we cannot do better.
